content.text: Gravity is the force of attraction between any two masses in the universe. It is a universal force that acts between all objects with mass.

content.name: Law of Gravitation
content.note: F $\propto$ product of masses; F $\propto$ 1/r². G $\approx$ 6.67 $\times$ 10$^{-11}$ Nm²/kg².

content.name: Gravity Formula
content.note: M = mass of Earth, R = radius of Earth. Value: g $\approx$ 9.8 m/s².

content.text: g decreases as height increases ($g_h = g(1 - 2h/R)$) and as depth increases ($g_d = g(1 - d/R)$). Due to rotation, g is maximum at poles and minimum at equator.

content.name: Potential & Intensity
content.note: V is Gravitational Potential (J/kg); E is Field Intensity (N/kg).

content.name: Velocity Formulas
content.note: Escape velocity ($v_e$) is the minimum speed to escape Earth's gravity. Orbital velocity ($v_o$) keeps a satellite in orbit.

content.text: 1. Planets move in elliptical orbits with the Sun at one focus. 2. Equal areas are swept in equal time. 3. $T^2 \propto R^3$ (T = time period, R = orbital radius).
